facts: The system let users register via email/password with email verification before login is allowed.
The system allow users to build a profile with a profile picture, cover photo, bio, and location.
The system let users create text posts with optional single or multi-image upload, and edit/delete their own posts.
The system let users like a post (toggle like/unlike) and see a simple like count.
The system let users comment on posts (flat, non-nested) and edit/delete their own comments.
The system show a basic news feed listing posts from friends/followed users sorted by most recent first (simple chronological query).
The system let users send and accept/reject friend requests, with a friends list page.
The system let users follow/unfollow other users without requiring mutual approval, with a followers/following count.
The system let users create Groups with a name, description, and public/private flag, and join/leave public groups directly.
The system let group creators approve or reject join requests for private groups.
The system show a dedicated group feed listing only posts made within that group.
The system provide a basic direct messaging feature: 1:1 text conversations stored and retrieved like a simple chat log (no real-time typing indicators or read receipts).
The system build a notifications list (likes, comments, friend requests) generated on action and marked read/unread on click.
The system implement basic keyword search across users, posts, and groups using simple text matching (LIKE/contains query, no full-text engine required).
The system let users bookmark/save posts into a single default 'Saved Posts' list (no custom collections).
The system provide a 'Report Post' button that creates a report record with a reason dropdown, viewable in an admin list.
The system build a simple admin panel where admins can view reported content and delete a post or suspend a user account (boolean active/suspended flag).
The system let users set basic privacy on their profile (public or private) controlling whether non-friends can view their posts.
The system let users block another user, which hides each other's posts and prevents messaging.
The system let users update notification preferences with on/off toggles per category (likes, comments, friend requests).
The system let admins manually grant or revoke a 'verified' badge on a user account (boolean flag toggle).
The system let users view their own post history and edit account settings (email, password, username).
The system implement basic pagination/infinite scroll on feeds, comments, and search results (limit/offset queries).
The system let users delete their own account, which soft-deletes their data (sets a deleted flag rather than hard deletion).
The system enforce two roles only — Admin and User — with simple route guards (no granular permission matrix), and log admin actions (ban/delete/verify) to a basic activity log table.

evidence.claim: The system enables users to create both public and private groups, and manage membership requests for private ones.
evidence.fact: The system let users create Groups with a name, description, and public/private flag, and join/leave public groups directly.
